compiled_state.title: commit workflow: deep tier reviewers use general-purpose subagent type instead of named reviewer agent subagent types
compiled_state.author: Test

comments.body: Bug details: During the commit workflow's deep tier review phase, the orchestrator dispatches reviewers using subagent_type='general-purpose' instead of the named reviewer subagent types. The DSO plugin defines named agents (code-reviewer-deep-correctness, code-reviewer-deep-verification, code-reviewer-deep-hygiene, code-reviewer-deep-arch) in plugins/dso/agents/. These should be used directly rather than dispatching general-purpose agents with the agent file content pasted into the prompt. The correct approach per CLAUDE.md: read the agent file inline and use it as the task prompt with subagent_type='general-purpose' and the agent's specified model. NOTE: This is actually the correct approach since dso:* values are not valid subagent_type values — but the LLM-behavioral bug is that the orchestrator does not follow the inline-dispatch pattern properly, and instead constructs its own prompt structure that deviates from the agent's instructions. Fix: always read plugins/dso/agents/<reviewer>.md inline and use its content verbatim as the Agent task prompt.
comments.author: Test
